creator: stephen@math.missouri.edu
text:
I have a feeling that it was dumb of me to send this PR.  I had relied
on people who told me that there were not similar problems with the
Linux version.  But the other day I tried mozilla on Windows, and it did
not display the fonts properly (although it did not crash).  So I
suspect that this is a mozilla issue, not a FreeBSD issue.  So:
This PR can be closed.

I had written:

     mozilla dies with segmentation fault at the web page 
     http://www.math.missouri.edu/~stephen/preprints/
     and freezes with the web page
     http://www.math.missouri.edu/~stephen/junk/
     mozilla-0.9.1 did display the pages, but failed to display the
symbol fonts properly.

     These are not strange web pages - the only unusual thing about them
is that they
     contain <font face=symbol>.

     I asked on a mozilla newsgroup, and apparently the linux version
displays these
     pages properly.

-- 
Stephen Montgomery-Smith
stephen@math.missouri.edu
http://www.math.missouri.edu/~stephen
